prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Alright dear, imagine the CPU as the brain of the computer. Just like how our brain tells our body what to do, the CPU tells the computer how to work. It's a very tiny but super important part that helps the computer do all sorts of things, like play games or show videos. People say it can think really fast, much faster than us! Even though I don't know all the details, it's fascinating how it helps make computers work, isn’t it?
